title: Query-group ranker for within-sequence ordering, followed by OOF calibration
summary: ROC AUC rewards ranking, and learning-to-rank objectives explicitly optimize pair ordering within groups.
rationale: ROC AUC rewards ranking, and learning-to-rank objectives explicitly optimize pair ordering within groups. In this problem, the natural groups are driver-race or driver-race-stint sequences, where the model mainly needs to rank which laps are the most pit-imminent.
implementation_hint: Train an `XGBRanker` or CatBoost pairwise ranker with `qid` equal to driver-race or driver-race-stint, compare `rank:pairwise` or `rank:ndcg` against binary GBDT, then calibrate the rank scores into probabilities with grouped out-of-fold isotonic, sigmoid, or temperature scaling.
expected_effect: Better intra-sequence ordering of imminent pit laps, especially when positives are rare but locally concentrated.
risk: Ranker outputs are not probabilities and can hurt submission quality if calibration is not fully out-of-fold; grouping choice also matters and can underperform if too coarse or too small.
